subject: Islamiat
chapter_name: Iman and Aqaid — Faith and Beliefs

note_data.title: Iman and Aqaid — Faith and Beliefs
keyPoints: Iman means to believe with heart, to confess with tongue and to act with limbs
Six articles of Iman (Arkan-e-Iman): Belief in Allah, Angels, Books, Prophets, Day of Judgment, and Taqdeer
Tawheed means the Oneness and Uniqueness of Allah in His essence, attributes and actions
Angels are created from Noor (light) and are sinless beings who always obey Allah's commands
Four revealed books: Torah (Tawrat), Psalms (Zabur), Gospel (Injeel), and Quran
124,000 Prophets were sent by Allah, with 25 mentioned by name in the Quran
Akhirah includes death, grave, resurrection, accountability, and eternal life in Paradise or Hell
Taqdeer means Allah's predestination - everything happens according to Allah's knowledge and will
Shirk is the greatest sin in Islam - associating partners with Allah
A true believer must have firm faith in all six articles without any doubt

definitions.definition: To believe with heart, to confess with tongue and to act with limbs according to the teachings of Islam
The belief in the Oneness and Uniqueness of Allah in His essence, attributes and actions
To associate partners with Allah or to worship anyone or anything besides Allah
The divine predestination - Allah's eternal knowledge and decree about everything that will happen
The life after death which includes resurrection, judgment, and eternal life in Paradise or Hell

importantQuestions.question: Define Iman and write its three components.
importantQuestions.answer: Iman means to believe with heart, to confess with tongue and to act with limbs. Its three components are: 1) Belief with heart 2) Confession with tongue 3) Action with limbs.

importantQuestions.question: Write a detailed note on the six articles of Iman (Arkan-e-Iman).
importantQuestions.answer: The six articles of Iman are: 1) Belief in Allah - He is One, Creator and Sustainer 2) Belief in Angels - created from light, sinless, obey Allah 3) Belief in Books - Torah, Zabur, Injeel, Quran 4) Belief in Prophets - 124,000 sent, last was Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) 5) Belief in Day of Judgment - resurrection and accountability 6) Belief in Taqdeer - Allah's predestination and divine decree

mcqs.question: How many articles of Iman are there?
The greatest sin in Islam is:

commonMistakes: Students often forget to mention all three components of Iman definition
Mixing up the order of six articles of Iman
Writing 'Taqdeer' as 'fate' instead of divine predestination
Not mentioning that Angels are created from Noor (light)
Forgetting that there are 124,000 Prophets in total
examTips: This chapter usually has 2-3 MCQs and 1 long question in every BSEK paper
Always write the exact definition of Iman as given in textbook
Memorize all six articles of Iman in correct order
Questions about Shirk and its consequences appear frequently
Write Arabic terms with their English meanings in brackets
note_data.keyDiagram: Six Articles of Iman diagram showing interconnected circles with: Allah, Angels, Books, Prophets, Day of Judgment, and Taqdeer - commonly asked to draw and label
